Okay, so I am super excited about this presentation because it is something I see so often in the group, which is the idea of being criticized. And I would call this presentation how to have thick skin if I could, but I feel like it wouldn't be as appealing. But this is something that I hold really close to my heart just because when I was younger I don't think that I was a person who had very thick skin at all. I think I was easily put down by others. I let everything people say get to my head. And in order to run a business you cannot be that person because you will crumble. And so what I want to welcome you to as an idea is that you are constantly now being critiqued and you need to get used to it. Okay, so what you must realize is that people will always criticize what you do and what you don't. That is now part of your job. Literally in your job description it should say always going to be criticized because you will. Whether you make it, you might think that you made the best decision for your business and you might be like there's no way that people and people will be like I don't know what the fuck the CEO is doing. You know I don't know why I work for this dude. What an idiot. You know and it's just that is part of the job and so you need to accept that now. Okay, and the more success that you gain the more of these critics are gonna come out of the woodworks. As you have, when you have five things that happen that are successful you're gonna have five people that come out that are critics. As you gain more success and you do 100 amazing things you're gonna have 100 people coming and hating on you. Okay, that's just the law of success. It's like when you have it with equal positive reaction comes equal negative reaction right and the more success you have the more people will also not like you hate you etc. Right, and that is because people having natural tendency to cut down those who grow. Why do people do this? Because it makes them feel small and insecure. Everything that you are doing is representing what they believe is not possible and therefore they want to cut you down and say that it's because you lied, cheated, stole, whatever because then it makes them feel better off the fact that they are not doing what you are doing and they're not getting outside of their comfort zone. Okay, so you must understand is that you cannot avoid criticism. You have to learn how to lead boldly through it. You cannot avoid it. You cannot dismiss it. You cannot hide from it. You have to confront it head-on and you have to do that in front of your team. Okay, so how do you go about reducing the noise of the critics around you? Right, number one and most important is you build a positive culture that starts with yourself. Okay, you set the tone for your subordinates. The people under you are going to follow what you do. So if you are taken aback or upset about something that a client says or something that someone on Facebook says then expect your team to be five times more upset when it happens. All right, instead you need to believe the best in people rather than the worst. Okay, so every time I see a negative comment on Facebook because believe me, I've gotten everything you can think of. I think to myself, man, that person's probably got to have so much going on that they would take the time to tell me how ugly I am and how much of a scammer I am and how horrible my business is. Like they really have to feel like shit about themselves. Okay, and you know, they're probably not a bad person. They're probably just going through a really hard time because that's probably the truth. I don't think most people intend to be bad. I think that most people don't know how to be happy and therefore when they see others that are happy or successful, they feel really bad about themselves and want to cut you down. Okay, second is you need to be helpful rather than harsh, meaning that when you are looking at all of this, you need to think how this could be helpful rather than it could hurt you. Okay, and lastly is you need to lead from abundance and not fear. Meaning if you hear one client says something or one person on Facebook says something, like you cannot freak out. You cannot freak out. I see so many people in the group that I got this bad review. What the fuck do I do? It's like you accept the fact that you have a bad review and if somebody asks you about it, you confront it. Like go look at my last door. Ask me if I care. Like as you have more success, more people will not like you. I am very okay with the fact that I will have a lot of people who do not like me in life and that's okay because I like me and that's what matters. Alright. Number two, create countless avenues for helpful feedback. Alright, what do I mean by that? It's up to you how feedback is given and interpreted. Okay, and so as you're growing your business, you are going to have critics from the inside and those are going to be the ones that hurt the most. And so you might as well be proactive about it because people do not like people who don't receive feedback well. Right? If they can't tell you what they think about the business and the decisions you're making, they're going to be doing it elsewhere and behind your back and it rather wouldn't you rather like have it be done in front of you and you be aware of what's going on. Right? Okay, so you want to keep creative culture where it's given a helpful way rather than a harmful way. Alright, so it means teaching people how to give feedback, what's appropriate. You know, it's not appropriate to be mean or angry, but like giving useful feedback is something that you have to model so that your team knows how to do it. Right? An example I could give you is a Facebook group. Right? For us in our Facebook group, I have told our team many times I don't want you to delete something just because you think it's quote unquote negative because it might be saying something about our business that you know we're not doing something well. I would like it there because I use that group as feedback. So I'm looking in there to see like what are we doing well? What could we be doing better? What are we not doing well? Right? And so I tell the team that and then they're like, oh, okay, it's okay for people to say if they're not happy with something. It's okay for people to say if that we could be doing something better. Right? And so then that's just you have to remember it starts with the little things. So it starts with how you receive feedback from clients, how you receive feedback from people on the outside. And lastly, that leads to how you receive feedback with your team. Right? And so you need to learn from those who care about you on the inside, meaning your team, so that you aren't taken down by those who don't give a crap about you on the outside. Okay? Your team sees a lot of what you don't see. And so though they don't have often the same perspective you have as the CEO and business owner, they could probably tell you where your flaws lie and where the business is most vulnerable. And it's important that you listen to that. Okay? Because you can't have a business without people. Three is leading with the why. Okay? So as leaders, we are changing. Meaning we're constantly changing things. We're constantly the ones who are saying, I get that you like doing it this way. And I get that we just did this like two weeks ago, but like, we have to change again. Right? And the thing is, most people resist change because they aren't informed. Right? They just don't understand why you actually have to make the change. It's like coming in and saying we need to raise our prices. And they're like, okay, why? Because you want to take on more money? Grief bastard. You know what I mean? Like, they're not going to take that well. You have to explain why. Like digging in, showing them your financial statement saying, looking at this, like the last three months, we've lost money. And so it really doesn't make sense with this price point. In fact, look at the math. I broke it down to you. How many people we would need to actually even make a profit at this price is not, it's not fathomable. We can't even run enough classes. Right? You have to break it down to math, physics, whatever it may be when you're leading your team through understanding a change like this. Right? And it's like, people don't resist change because they don't like it. They resist change because they don't understand it. And so you have to be very patient and you have to repeat yourself a lot. Whenever we are making a big change in the business, we have multiple meetings about it. We have multiple communications that go out about it. We ask and provide lots of time for people ask questions about it. Okay? Because you want to make sure people can wrap their head around it. Otherwise, what they will do is resist it and they won't pay attention to it and they're going to try and keep everything how it is. Okay? And so what will informing them of that? Why do it will disarm critics? So the people that are criticizing you will be disarmed. It will educate by standards. The people who aren't sure how to feel are going to get educated so they can make an informed decision and it will empower advocates. Okay? The people who always stand behind you are going to be like, that's why we believe in you. That's why we trust you, etc. Right? Because you're giving them insight into why you're doing things. And last but not least is being wise and how you live. Okay? You have to accept that you are held to a higher standard as a business owner. You cannot do anything behind closed doors that you wouldn't do in front of your team. And you can't avoid shortcuts. You need to be selective in how you speak. So what I mean by all of this is that whether you like it or not, people are always watching you. Like I said in one of my prior videos, you are heard through a microphone and you are seen under a microscope. And so everything you do needs to be something you're proud of. And if you don't like how that sounds, then you shouldn't own a business. Because in order to lead people, you must be worthy of being a leader, meaning what do you what does your personal life look like? Like if your personal life is a mess, if you've got a crappy relationship, if you're overweight, like if you're not taking care of yourself, that is all reflected on how your team sees you. Okay? It's like how many people work with a personal trainer who's overweight? Like if you're trying to lose weight, are you going to work with a person who's overweight or you're going to work with a person who's in shape and like doing well? It's the same as being led. Like people want to follow a leader who's inspiring. You have to be an inspiring person. It's part of the job now. So like I said, if you don't like how that sounds or you don't want to believe it to be true, you're choosing ignorance and you should not own a business. And so I know that that sounds rough. But again, that is the truth.
